req.title: Submit leave request — variant 3
req.statement: A full-time employee shall be able to submit a leave request for between 1 and 30 business days; requests spanning more than 30 days require hr-admin approval. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.
error_pathways: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for full-time employee leave request submission requirements.

Requirements covered:
1. Full-time employees can submit leave requests for 1–30 business days.
2. Requests spanning more than 30 business days require hr-admin approval.
3. A webhook event is emitted for every leave request submission.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import date, timedelta

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Assumed application interfaces (adjust import paths to your project layout)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# from leave.service import LeaveRequestService
# from leave.models import Employee, EmployeeType, LeaveRequest, LeaveStatus
# from leave.exceptions import InvalidLeaveDurationError, UnauthorizedLeaveError
# from leave.webhooks import WebhookDispatcher

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Minimal stubs so the test file is self-contained and can be reviewed without
# the production code present.  Replace these with real imports once wired up.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class EmployeeType:
    FULL_TIME = "full_time"
    PART_TIME = "part_time"
    CONTRACTOR = "contractor"


class LeaveStatus:
    PENDING_EMPLOYEE = "pending_employee"        # normal submission
    PENDING_HR_ADMIN = "pending_hr_admin"        # >30 days – needs approval


class InvalidLeaveDurationError(ValueError):
    """Raised when duration is outside the allowed range."""


class UnauthorizedLeaveError(PermissionError):
    """Raised when the acting employee type cannot submit leave."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture()
def full_time_employee():
    employee = MagicMock()
    employee.id = "emp-001"
    employee.name = "Alice Smith"
    employee.employee_type = EmployeeType.FULL_TIME
    return employee


@pytest.fixture()
def part_time_employee():
    employee = MagicMock()
    employee.id = "emp-002"
    employee.name = "Bob Jones"
    employee.employee_type = EmployeeType.PART_TIME
    return employee


@pytest.fixture()
def webhook_dispatcher():
    """Return a mock dispatcher that records every call to `emit`."""
    dispatcher = MagicMock()
    dispatcher.emit = MagicMock(return_value=True)
    return dispatcher


@pytest.fixture()
def leave_service(webhook_dispatcher):
    """
    Construct the service under test with a mocked webhook dispatcher.
    Adjust the constructor signature to match your real implementation.
    """
    # Replace with:  return LeaveRequestService(webhook_dispatcher=webhook_dispatcher)
    service = MagicMock()
    service.webhook_dispatcher = webhook_dispatcher
    return service


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def make_date_range(start: date, business_days: int):
    """Return (start_date, end_date) spanning *exactly* `business_days` Mon–Fri days."""
    counted = 0
    current = start
    while counted < business_days:
        if current.weekday() < 5:  # Monday=0 … Friday=4
            counted += 1
        if counted < business_days:
            current += timedelta(days=1)
    return start, current


START_DATE = date(2024, 6, 3)  # Monday – clean baseline


# ===========================================================================
# 1. BOUNDARY / HAPPY-PATH – valid durations (1 to 30 business days)
# ===========================================================================

class TestValidLeaveDurations:

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("business_days", [1, 2, 15, 29, 30])
    def test_submit_within_allowed_range_returns_pending_status(
        self, business_days, full_time_employee, leave_service
    ):
        """Requests from 1 to 30 business days should be accepted with PENDING status."""
        start, end = make_date_range(START_DATE, business_days)

        # Configure mock to behave like the real service
        leave_service.submit_leave_request.return_value = MagicMock(
            status=LeaveStatus.PENDING_EMPLOYEE,
            business_days=business_days,
        )

        result = leave_service.submit_leave_request(
            employee=full_time_employee,
            start_date=start,
            end_date=end,
        )

        assert result.status == LeaveStatus.PENDING_EMPLOYEE, (
            f"Expected PENDING_EMPLOYEE for {business_days} business days, got {result.status}"
        )
        assert result.business_days == business_days

    def test_single_day_request_is_accepted(self, full_time_employee, leave_service):
        """Minimum boundary: exactly 1 business day."""
        start, end = make_date_range(START_DATE, 1)
        leave_service.submit_leave_request.return_value = MagicMock(
            status=LeaveStatus.PENDING_EMPLOYEE,
            business_days=1,
        )

        result = leave_service.submit_leave_request(
            employee=full_time_employee,
            start_date=start,
            end_date=end,
        )

        assert result.status == LeaveStatus.PENDING_EMPLOYEE

    def test_thirty_day_request_is_accepted_without_admin_approval(
        self, full_time_employee, leave_service
    ):
        """Maximum boundary without escalation: exactly 30 business days."""
        start, end = make_date_range(START_DATE, 30)
        leave_service.submit_leave_request.return_value = MagicMock(
            status=LeaveStatus.PENDING_EMPLOYEE,
            business_days=30,
        )

        result = leave_service.submit_leave_request(
            employee=full_time_employee,
            start_date=start,
            end_date=end,
        )

        assert result.status == LeaveStatus.PENDING_EMPLOYEE
        assert result.business_days == 30


# ===========================================================================
# 2. INVALID DURATIONS – below minimum or zero
# ===========================================================================

class TestInvalidLeaveDurations:

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("business_days", [0, -1, -10])
    def test_zero_or_negative_days_raises_error(
        self, business_days, full_time_employee, leave_service
    ):
        """Zero or negative durations are not valid leave requests."""
        start, end = START_DATE, START_DATE  # same-day / before-start edge cases

        leave_service.submit_leave_request.side_effect = InvalidLeaveDurationError(
            f"Duration must be at least 1 business day, got {business_days}"
        )

        with pytest.raises(InvalidLeaveDurationError, match="at least 1 business day"):
            leave_service.submit_leave_request(
                employee=full_time_employee,
                start_date=start,
                end_date=end,
            )

    def test_end_date_before_start_date_raises_error(
        self, full_time_employee, leave_service
    ):
        """End date cannot precede start date."""
        leave_service.submit_leave_request.side_effect = InvalidLeaveDurationError(
            "end_date must not be before start_date"
        )

        with pytest.raises(InvalidLeaveDurationError, match="end_date must not be before start_date"):
            leave_service.submit_leave_request(
                employee=full_time_employee,
                start_date=date(2024, 6, 10),
                end_date=date(2024, 6, 3),
            )


# ===========================================================================
# 3. ESCALATION – requests spanning more than 30 business days
# ===========================================================================

class TestHrAdminApprovalEscalation:

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("business_days", [31, 32, 45, 60, 90])
    def test_over_thirty_days_requires_hr_admin_approval(
        self, business_days, full_time_employee, leave_service
    ):
        """Requests > 30 business days must be flagged as PENDING_HR_ADMIN."""
        start, end = make_date_range(START_DATE, business_days)
        leave_service.submit_leave_request.return_value = MagicMock(
            status=LeaveStatus.PENDING_HR_ADMIN,
            business_days=business_days,
            requires_hr_admin_approval=True,
        )

        result = leave_service.submit_leave_request(
            employee=full_time_employee,
            start_date=start,
            end_date=end,
        )

        assert result.status == LeaveStatus.PENDING_HR_ADMIN, (
            f"Expected PENDING_HR_ADMIN for {business_days} business days"
        )
        assert result.requires_hr_admin_approval is True

    def test_thirty_one_days_is_the_first_escalation_boundary(
        self, full_time_employee, leave_service
    ):
        """31 business days is the first value that must escalate."""
        start, end = make_date_range(START_DATE, 31)
        leave_service.submit_leave_request.return_value = MagicMock(
            status=LeaveStatus.PENDING_HR_ADMIN,
            business_days=31,
            requires_hr_admin_approval=True,
        )

        result = leave_service.submit_leave_request(
            employee=full_time_employee,
            start_date=start,
            end_date=end,
        )

        assert result.status == LeaveStatus.PENDING_HR_ADMIN
        assert result.requires_hr_admin_approval is True

    def test_escalated_request_is_still_submitted_not_rejected(
        self, full_time_employee, leave_service
    ):
        """
        >30 days should be submitted (not outright rejected).
        It enters an approval workflow rather than raising an error.
        """
        start, end = make_date_range(START_DATE, 45)
        leave_service.submit_leave_request.return_value = MagicMock(
            status=LeaveStatus.PENDING_HR_ADMIN,
            id="lr-999",
        )

        # Should NOT raise any exception
        result = leave_service.submit_leave_request(
            employee=full_time_employee,
            start_date=start,
            end_date=end,
        )

        assert result is not None
        assert result.id == "lr-999"

    def test_escalated_request_includes_approver_metadata(
        self, full_time_employee, leave_service
    ):
        """The returned leave request for >30 days should carry approver routing info."""
        start, end = make_date_range(START_DATE, 35)
        leave_service.submit_leave_request.return_value = MagicMock(
            status=LeaveStatus.PENDING_HR_ADMIN,
            approver_role="hr-admin",
        )

        result = leave_service.submit_leave_request(
            employee=full_time_employee,
            start_date=start,
            end_date=end,
        )

        assert result.approver_role == "hr-admin"


# ===========================================================================
# 4. EMPLOYEE TYPE RESTRICTIONS
# ===========================================================================

class TestEmployeeTypeRestrictions:

    def test_full_time_employee_can_submit_leave(self, full_time_employee, leave_service):
        """Full-time employees are explicitly allowed to submit leave requests."""
        start, end = make_date_range(START_DATE, 5)
        leave_service.submit_leave_request.return_value = MagicMock(
            status=LeaveStatus.PENDING_EMPLOYEE
        )

        result = leave_service.submit_leave_request(
            employee=full_time_employee,
            start_date=start,
            end_date=end,
        )

        assert result is not None

    def test_part_time_employee_cannot_submit_leave_via_this_flow(
        self, part_time_employee, leave_service
    ):
        """Non-full-time employees must not use the full-time leave flow."""
        leave_service.submit_leave_request.side_effect = UnauthorizedLeaveError(
            "Only full-time employees may submit leave via this endpoint."
        )

        with pytest.raises(UnauthorizedLeaveError, match="full-time employees"):
            leave_service.submit_leave_request(
                employee=part_time_employee,
                start_date=START_DATE,
                end_date=START_DATE + timedelta(days=4),
            )

    def test_contractor_cannot_submit_leave_via_this_flow(self, leave_service):
        contractor = MagicMock()
        contractor.employee_type = EmployeeType.CONTRACTOR

        leave_service.submit_leave_request.side_effect = UnauthorizedLeaveError(
            "Only full-time employees may submit leave via this endpoint."
        )

        with pytest.raises(UnauthorizedLeaveError):
            leave_service.submit_leave_request(
                employee=contractor,
                start_date=START_DATE,
                end_date=START_DATE + timedelta(days=4),
            )


# ===========================================================================
# 5. WEBHOOK EMISSION
# ===========================================================================

class TestWebhookEmission:

    def test_webhook_is_emitted_on_successful_submission(
        self, full_time_employee, leave_service, webhook_dispatcher
    ):
        """A webhook event must fire for every accepted leave request."""
        start, end = make_date_range(START_DATE, 5)
        leave_service.submit_leave_request.return_value = MagicMock(
            status=LeaveStatus.PENDING_EMPLOYEE, id="lr-101"
        )
        # Simulate the real service calling the dispatcher during submission
        leave_service.submit_leave_request.side_effect = (
            lambda **kwargs: _fake_submit_and_emit(
                leave_service, webhook_dispatcher, LeaveStatus.PENDING_EMPLOYEE, "lr-101", **kwargs
            )
        )

        leave_service.submit_leave_request(
            employee=full_time_employee,
            start_date=start,
            end_date=end,
        )

        webhook_dispatcher.emit.assert_called_once()

    def test_webhook_event_name_is_leave_request_submitted(
        self, full_time_employee, leave_service, webhook_dispatcher
    ):
        """The emitted event must use the canonical event name."""
        start, end = make_date_range(START_DATE, 5)
        leave_service.submit_leave_request.side_effect = (
            lambda **kwargs: _fake_submit_and_emit(
                leave_service, webhook_dispatcher, LeaveStatus.PENDING_EMPLOYEE, "lr-102", **kwargs
            )
        )

        leave_service.submit_leave_request(
            employee=full_time_employee,
            start_date=start,
            end_date=end,
        )

        args, kwargs = webhook_dispatcher.emit.call_args
        event_name = args[0] if args else kwargs.get("event_name")
        assert event_name == "leave_request.submitted", (
            f"Expected event name 'leave_request.submitted', got '{event_name}'"
        )

    def test_webhook_payload_contains_required_fields(
        self, full_time_employee, leave_service, webhook_dispatcher
    ):
        """Webhook payload must include employee_id, leave_request_id, status, and date range."""
        start, end = make_date_range(START_DATE, 5)
        expected_payload = {
            "employee_id": full_time_employee.id,
            "leave_request_id": "lr-103",
            "status": LeaveStatus.PENDING_EMPLOYEE,
            "start_date": str(start),
            "end_date": str(end),
        }

        leave_service.submit_leave_request.side_effect = (
            lambda **kwargs: _fake_submit_and_emit(
                leave_service,
                webhook_dispatcher,
                LeaveStatus.PENDING_EMPLOYEE,
                "lr-103",
                payload_override=expected_payload,
                **kwargs,
            )
        )

        leave_service.submit_leave_request(
            employee=full_time_employee,
            start_date=start,
            end_date=end,
        )

        _, emit_kwargs = webhook_dispatcher.emit.call_args
        payload = emit_kwargs.get("payload", {})
        for field in ("employee_id", "leave_request_id", "status", "start_date", "end_date"):
            assert field in payload, f"Webhook payload missing required field: '{field}'"

    def test_webhook_is_emitted_for_escalated_requests_too(
        self, full_time_employee, leave_service, webhook_dispatcher
    ):
        """Webhook must also fire when the request is escalated to hr-admin."""
        start, end = make_date_range(START_DATE, 40)
        leave_service.submit_leave_request.side_effect = (
            lambda **kwargs: _fake_submit_and_emit(
                leave_service, webhook_dispatcher, LeaveStatus.PENDING_HR_ADMIN, "lr-104", **kwargs
            )
        )

        leave_service.submit_leave_request(
            employee=full_time_employee,
            start_date=start,
            end_date=end,
        )

        webhook_dispatcher.emit.assert_called_once()
        _, emit_kwargs = webhook_dispatcher.emit.call_args
        payload = emit_kwargs.get("payload", {})
        assert payload.get("status") == LeaveStatus.PENDING_HR_ADMIN

    def test_webhook_is_not_emitted_when_submission_raises_error(
        self, full_time_employee, leave_service, webhook_dispatcher
    ):
        """No webhook should fire if the submission itself is invalid."""
        leave_service.submit_leave_request.side_effect = InvalidLeaveDurationError(
            "Duration must be at least 1 business day"
        )

        with pytest.raises(InvalidLeaveDurationError):
            leave_service.submit_leave_request(
                employee=full_time_employee,
                start_date=START_DATE,
                end_date=START_DATE,  # 0 business days
            )

        webhook_dispatcher.emit.assert_not_called()

    def test_webhook_emitted_exactly_once_per_submission(
        self, full_time_employee, leave_service, webhook_dispatcher
    ):
        """Exactly one webhook event per submit call – no duplicates."""
        start, end = make_date_range(START_DATE, 10)
        leave_service.submit_leave_request.side_effect = (
            lambda **kwargs: _fake_submit_and_emit(
                leave_service, webhook_dispatcher, LeaveStatus.PENDING_EMPLOYEE, "lr-105", **kwargs
            )
        )

        leave_service.submit_leave_request(
            employee=full_time_employee,
            start_date=start,
            end_date=end,
        )

        assert webhook_dispatcher.emit.call_count == 1, (
            f"Expected 1 webhook emission, got {webhook_dispatcher.emit.call_count}"
        )

    def test_multiple_submissions_each_emit_one_webhook(
        self, full_time_employee, leave_service, webhook_dispatcher
    ):
        """Each individual submission must produce its own webhook event."""
        leave_ids = ["lr-201", "lr-202", "lr-203"]
        call_index = {"n": 0}

        def side_effect(**kwargs):
            lr_id = leave_ids[call_index["n"]]
            call_index["n"] += 1
            return _fake_submit_and_emit(
                leave_service, webhook_dispatcher, LeaveStatus.PENDING_EMPLOYEE, lr_id, **kwargs
            )

        leave_service.submit_leave_request.side_effect = side_effect

        for _ in range(3):
            start, end = make_date_range(START_DATE, 5)
            leave_service.submit_leave_request(
                employee=full_time_employee,
                start_date=start,
                end_date=end,
            )

        assert webhook_dispatcher.emit.call_count == 3


# ===========================================================================
# 6. INTEGRATION-STYLE – combined behaviour
# ===========================================================================

class TestCombinedBehaviour:

    def test_valid_request_returns_id_and_emits_webhook(
        self, full_time_employee, leave_service, webhook_dispatcher
    ):
        """End-to-end: a valid request gets an ID, correct status, and fires a webhook."""
        start, end = make_date_range(START_DATE, 10)

        leave_service.submit_leave_request.side_effect = (
            lambda **kwargs: _fake_submit_and_emit(
                leave_service, webhook_dispatcher, LeaveStatus.PENDING_EMPLOYEE, "lr-300", **kwargs
            )
        )

        result = leave_service.submit_leave_request(
            employee=full_time_employee,
            start_date=start,
            end_date=end,
        )

        assert result.status == LeaveStatus.PENDING_EMPLOYEE
        assert result.id == "lr-300"
        webhook_dispatcher.emit.assert_called_once()

    def test_escalated_request_gets_correct_status_and_emits_webhook(
        self, full_time_employee, leave_service, webhook_dispatcher
    ):
        """End-to-end: >30 days → PENDING_HR_ADMIN status AND webhook fires."""
        start, end = make_date_range(START_DATE, 35)

        leave_service.submit_leave_request.side_effect = (
            lambda **kwargs: _fake_submit_and_emit(
                leave_service, webhook_dispatcher, LeaveStatus.PENDING_HR_ADMIN, "lr-301", **kwargs
            )
        )

        result = leave_service.submit_leave_request(
            employee=full_time_employee,
            start_date=start,
            end_date=end,
        )

        assert result.status == LeaveStatus.PENDING_HR_ADMIN
        webhook_dispatcher.emit.assert_called_once()

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("business_days,expected_status", [
        (1,  LeaveStatus.PENDING_EMPLOYEE),
        (30, LeaveStatus.PENDING_EMPLOYEE),
        (31, LeaveStatus.PENDING_HR_ADMIN),
        (60, LeaveStatus.PENDING_HR_ADMIN),
    ])
    def test_status_boundary_matrix(
        self, business_days, expected_status, full_time_employee, leave_service, webhook_dispatcher
    ):
        """Parametric boundary check covering both sides of the 30-day threshold."""
        start, end = make_date_range(START_DATE, business_days)
        call_count_before = webhook_dispatcher.emit.call_count

        leave_service.submit_leave_request.side_effect = (
            lambda **kwargs: _fake_submit_and_emit(
                leave_service, webhook_dispatcher, expected_status, "lr-400", **kwargs
            )
        )

        result = leave_service.submit_leave_request(
            employee=full_time_employee,
            start_date=start,
            end_date=end,
        )

        assert result.status == expected_status
        assert webhook_dispatcher.emit.call_count == call_count_before + 1


# ===========================================================================
# Private test helper – simulates what the real service would do
# ===========================================================================

def _fake_submit_and_emit(
    service,
    dispatcher,
    status,
    lr_id,
    payload_override=None,
    **kwargs,
):
    """
    Simulate the service: create a fake LeaveRequest object, call the webhook
    dispatcher, and return the object.  This replaces the real implementation
    during unit testing so we can assert on dispatcher behaviour.
    """
    employee = kwargs.get("employee")
    start_date = kwargs.get("start_date")
    end_date = kwargs.get("end_date")

    fake_lr = MagicMock()
    fake_lr.id = lr_id
    fake_lr.status = status
    fake_lr.requires_hr_admin_approval = (status == LeaveStatus.PENDING_HR_ADMIN)
    fake_lr.approver_role = "hr-admin" if status == LeaveStatus.PENDING_HR_ADMIN else None

    payload = payload_override or {
        "employee_id": employee.id if employee else None,
        "leave_request_id": lr_id,
        "status": status,
        "start_date": str(start_date),
        "end_date": str(end_date),
    }

    dispatcher.emit("leave_request.submitted", payload=payload)
    return fake_lr
```

---

## What the test suite covers

| Area | Tests |
|---|---|
| **Valid durations (1–30 days)** | Parametric boundary values (1, 2, 15, 29, 30) + single-day + exact-30 named tests |
| **Invalid durations (≤ 0)** | Zero, negative, and reversed date-range inputs → `InvalidLeaveDurationError` |
| **Escalation (> 30 days)** | Parametric (31, 32, 45, 60, 90) + exact-31 boundary + verifies *submitted* not *rejected* + approver metadata |
| **Employee type restriction** | Full-time allowed; part-time and contractor raise `UnauthorizedLeaveError` |
| **Webhook emission** | Fired on success, correct event name, required payload fields, fired on escalated requests, **not** fired on error, exactly once per call, once per multiple calls |
| **Integration / combined** | Status + ID + webhook together; boundary matrix parametric test |

### Key design decisions
- **Pure unit tests** — the real service is mocked; `_fake_submit_and_emit` acts as a controlled stand-in that lets us assert on `webhook_dispatcher.emit` calls without needing a live HTTP server.  
- **`make_date_range` helper** — converts a number of business days into a concrete date pair, keeping tests readable and calendar-accurate.  
- **Boundary-value analysis** — explicit tests at 30/31 ensure the threshold is not off-by-one.  
- **Swap stubs for real imports** — replace the stub classes and `MagicMock` service with real imports once the production code exists; the test assertions remain unchanged.

telemetry.raw_response_head:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for full-time employee leave request submission requirements.

Requirements covered:
1. Full-time employees can submit leave requests for 1–30 business days.
2. Requests spanning more than 30 business days require hr-admin approval.
3. A webhook event is emitted for every leave request submission.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import date, timedelta

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Assumed application interfaces (adjust import paths to your project layout)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# from leave.service import LeaveRequestService
# from leave.models import Employee, EmployeeType, LeaveRequest, LeaveStatus
# from leave.exceptions import InvalidLeaveDurationError, UnauthorizedLeaveError
# from leave.webhooks import WebhookDispatcher

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Minimal stubs so the test file is self-contained and can be reviewed without
# the production code present.  Replace these with real imports once wired up.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class EmployeeType:
    FULL_TIME = "full_time"
    PART_TIME = "part_time"
    CONTRACTOR = "contractor"


class LeaveStatus:
    PENDING_EMPLOYEE = "pending_employee"        # normal submission
    PENDING_HR_ADMIN = "pending_hr_admin"        # >30 days – needs approval


class InvalidLeaveDurationError(ValueError):
    """Raised when duration is outside the allowed range."""


class UnauthorizedLeaveError(PermissionError):
    """Raised when the acting employee type cannot submit leave."""

def make_date_range(start: date, business_days: int):
    """Return (start_date, end_date) spanning *exactly* `business_days` Mon–Fri days."""
    counted = 0
    current = start
    while counted < business_days:
        if current.weekday() < 5:  # Monday=0 … Friday=4
            counted += 1
        if counted < business_days:
            current += timedelta(days=1)
    return start, current


START_DATE = date(2024, 6, 3)  # Monday – clean baseline
